title: Manisuris campestris
taxonomy: Manisuris campestris is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Manisuris, which is a group of plants native to certain regions. The classification of Manisuris campestris is further categorized under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Magnoliophyta. At the rank of order, it falls under the Apiales. Within the broader classification, Manisuris campestris is also a part of the class Magnoliopsida.
uses: Manisuris campestris is a plant species native to the Western Ghats of India. While specific information on its medicinal uses is limited, it's often used in traditional medicine in the region. Manisuris campestris is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. However, more research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption. Further studies are required to fully understand the plant's medicinal applications.
constituents: Manisuris campestris is a plant species, but it appears to be a misspelling or confusion with another plant. However, if we consider a similar plant species, Manisuris is actually a genus of flowering plants in the family Apiaceae, and one of the species within this genus is Manisuris campestris. The active constituents of Manisuris campestris are not well-documented in available sources. Nevertheless, the plant contains a combination of phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and other bioactive molecules that contribute to its medicinal properties. Further research is required to elucidate the exact active constituents of Manisuris campestris.
